title: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) — Intrinsic Value & Prospect Score
description: Opus 4.6 independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD). Economic Prospect Score: 72/100 — Strong Prospect.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y10)
fcfGrowth.rationale: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. is projected to grow FCF at 18% annually based on competitive dynamics and historical performance.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A 11.0% WACC reflects Advanced Micro Devices Inc.'s risk profile, sector volatility, and balance sheet quality.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 3.5% terminal rate for long-run growth convergence toward nominal GDP.

thesisTitle: Advanced Micro Devices Inc.: A Fundamental Assessment
thesisParagraphs: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) is an S&P 500 constituent with established competitive advantages and consistent cash flow generation. The company's market position provides pricing power and earnings stability.

My DCF applies a 18% FCF growth rate reflecting the company's historical compounding ability, with a 11.0% discount rate capturing sector-specific risks. The valuation is sensitive to growth assumptions — investors should stress-test these inputs.
verdictFooter: Opus 4.6 independent analysis. Data via public filings (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: What is Advanced Micro Devices Inc.'s intrinsic value according to Opus?
faq.answer: Based on a 10-year DCF with 18% growth and 11.0% WACC, Opus values AMD at $134.62/share vs $175.00 current price.

faq.question: Is AMD overvalued or undervalued?
faq.answer: Opus's DCF suggests AMD is overvalued with a -23.1% margin of safety.

faq.question: Should I trade based on this?
faq.answer: No. This is not financial advice. DCF models are sensitive to assumptions. Use as one data point among many.

prospectVerdictDetail: AMD has executed one of the greatest turnarounds in semiconductor history under Lisa Su. The MI300 AI accelerator is gaining traction as a credible NVIDIA alternative. The CPU business continues to take share from Intel. But AMD remains a distant #2 in AI GPUs, and valuation assumes significant AI revenue growth that hasn't materialized at scale.
prospectKeyRisks: NVIDIA's CUDA ecosystem lock-in making MI300 adoption an uphill battle despite competitive hardware specs
Custom AI ASICs from hyperscalers potentially reducing the merchant GPU TAM that AMD targets
Intel Gaudi 3 and other competitors narrowing AMD's cost-performance advantage in AI inference
prospectKeyCatalysts: MI300X/MI350 gaining meaningful share (15-20%) of the AI GPU market as customers seek NVIDIA alternatives for supply diversification
Server CPU market share reaching 35%+ as Intel continues to stumble on process technology
Embedded and gaming segments recovering from cyclical lows, providing earnings diversification beyond data center
prospectMethodology: Score is based on three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30), totaling 0-100. Each pillar is broken into individually scored factors with transparent rationale. Data sources include FY2025 10-K filings, analyst consensus estimates, news sentiment analysis, and competitive landscape assessment. The score is forward-looking and represents economic prospect over a 2-3 year horizon.
